videos.title: The farmer's son became a Millionaire in Africa using 'The Shark Mentality'
videos.description:
From the dangerous mindset that no one is coming to save you to the revolutionary truth that when you realize at age 12 or 13 that your entire family is waiting for someone else to rescue them you get an awakening that changes everything, and why the brutal truth about becoming a millionaire at 25 and losing it all by 27 is that the first thing that comes to an average Ghanaian person's mind when given an opportunity in business is steal. A deep conversation about the mindset of success with Christian Amoh

00:00:00 Introduction: Meet Christian Zen Amo
00:03:17 The Awakening: Realizing No One Is Coming to Save You
00:10:42 Learning from Grandma: The Entrepreneurial Foundation
00:14:27 The Missionary Years: Finding Purpose and Building Skills
00:21:28 First Million: Breaking Into International Trade
00:28:20 The Rice Business Dream and Devastating Loss
00:33:45 The Dishonesty Problem: Why Ghanaian Entrepreneurs Struggle
00:38:12 Rising from the Ashes: The Comeback Strategy
00:43:49 The Rice Cartel: Understanding Ghana's Import Economy
00:48:28 Predatory Pricing and Market Competition
00:50:46 The Import vs Local Production Debate
00:55:12 Advice for Those Who Feel Stuck
00:57:35 The Shark Philosophy: Becoming Valuable to Winners
01:01:44 Final Wisdom: Discipline Over Motivation

Guest: Christian Zen Amoh
Company - Ohemaa Rice

Host: Derrick Abaitey
IG: https://www.instagram.com/derrick.abaitey
YT: https://www.youtube.com/@DerrickAbaitey

videos.title: How to Make Easy Money in Ghana | Ama Burland
videos.description:
In this raw episode of Konnected Minds, host Derrick Abaitey sits down with *Ama Burland,* the popular influencer and founder of Diya Organics who dismantles the dangerous wait until you're perfect before you post content mentality that keeps young people broke and afraid, revealing the exact moment when 10 bottles of hair oil she made for free and posted on YouTube sold out in 30 minutes after a scandal made her want to kill herself, when she struggled for two years not making money because she started with plenty orders and had to beg 20 customers when delivery services embarrassed her, when she shut down her skincare business because someone burned their face with African black soap they left on for 15 minutes instead of one minute.

00:00:00 Introduction: Meet Ama Burland
00:03:36 Growing Up: Family Background and Upbringing
00:09:57 School Days and Early Aspirations
00:17:36 The Main Character Mindset
00:21:10 Dealing with Bullying and Online Hate
00:32:00 Starting the Content Creation Journey
00:34:06 Birth of DL Organics: From Viral Moment to Business
00:51:49 Building a Business: Challenges and Growth
00:40:17 The Reality of Influencing: Money, Brands, and Sustainability
01:00:54 Advice for Young Entrepreneurs and Content Creators

Guest: Ama Burland
Business: https://www.shopdiyaorganics.com/shop

Host: Derrick Abaitey
IG: https://www.instagram.com/derrick.abaitey
YT: https://www.youtube.com/@DerrickAbaitey

a very good interview

All Ghananians, no matter his or her Status in life, have this "strange" mentality to live like a "Lord"(Broni). It became inherent in us. This is the effect of colonisation. So as soon as we get into a Position where he/she comes into contact with money, this "Lord" mentality clicks into place. And we tell ourselves "this is it!" So he/she takes the opportunity to "steal" some of the money to buy the latest clothes, shoes, eat "good" food. This happens at every level of any profession. From a factory worker, a messenger in an office, Taxi Driver, Politician, infact every Ghananian. Why do Businesses of "Rich" people collapse after their demise. A Kwahu Businessman I knew went everywhere with a Charted Car, he died, and the first thing his Wofasie who inherited him did was to buy the latest Benz on the Market! Also a driver I worked with in a government department, on retirement, had his daughter bought him a Taxi, to take care of himself, within a few months the Car broke down and he had no money to repair it -all because he lived like a Lord/Rich man.

Derrick, this interview is one of the best I’ve watched in 2026. You’re truly a great guy.

The politicians who are supposed to initiate support for local producers are the importers, why will they support local producers who are their competitors?!!!!

My dad in 2012 lost 40 hectares of rice to bushfire in Kintampo all because there was no combine harvestor. Guess what, the gov who supplied fertilizers even demanded for a paycheque knowing it was a disaster. 

Will we sit and watch for importation to be impossible because of global issues before we wake up as Ghanaians??

Canada relied on US for trade over years. Today with just one bite from Trump as a trading partner, the damage is not fathomable. 

This issue you just discussed is the most complex business issue in Gh now.

My brother owned a pharmaceutical company, and he liked sacking his employees. I thought he was the problem until one day he employed me at the workplace. He was not around most of the time, and trust me, I wished to sack all the employees and re-employ new ones… Herh! Right now I’m comfortable firing employees—sack them until you get the real ones. No compromise!!

I know that it is as a result of not associating myself with trends and not being active on social media. I have heard the name Ama Burland before, but have not paid any attention to it, just as I do with others. I have also heard about getting a mentor for one's self, but have not heeded. However, this podcast has given me a different perspective. This is my second time watching. The first was the one with Nana Aba which made me follow Women of Valour and this one with Ama Burland is my second. I loved every bit of the conversation. I laughed it off when I watched the teaser and heard her mention GH 50.00 as her start up money. I was like I have heard that before and laughed it off. But watching to the end, I have a different mindset now and although I am not younger than her, I choose her to be my mentor because I am free spirited as she is and love how she views things and want to be as successful as she is. Today marks the beginning of me rewriting my story. Thanks very much Konnected Minds, Derrick, and Ama Burland. Girl, you do all and I love you already🥰

The waiting till one can afford 3X the cost of something is an advice that should be directed to the state/the government (of GH). The average person (who eventually becomes politicians) should also have that awareness at the back of their mind. We tend to think the state (coffers) is an institution to exploit and make money from as opposed to the other way around. They embark on projects (most often with funds borrowed from the IMF) mainly for the procurement contract "benefit", without maintenance arrangements in place. The state has to be wealthier to be able to protect its citizens. I watched a video reel sometime back and listened to Kwame Nkrumah's speech during the inauguration ceremony of the Akosombo dam. That speech literally was the beginning of his end! He spoke from both sides of his mouth even as representatives of Kaiser Aluminum (an American company that had funded the dam) were in attendance. America got rid of him (thru the C IA) to "protect" an American company's investment.
